title: nolana carnosa
intro: Nolana carnosa, also known as Chilean shrubby nolana or Chilean trailing nolana, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Solanaceae. Traditionally, Nolana carnosa has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant's leaves and stems have been used to treat various health issues, such as wounds, fever, and respiratory problems, due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, offering potential health benefits like improved wound healing and reduced inflammation. Nolana carnosa is often used in herbal preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, although its effectiveness and safety for human consumption have not been extensively scientifically proven.
